summary: Matched the sysdolphin baselib gobjobject unit by replacing asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjobject.s with src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjobject.c and switching obj_files.mk to build the C object. The new C covers lookup by classifier within the r13 entity pointer lists, attaching object data to an HSD_GObj with an assert, detaching object data, and destroying attached object data via a kind-indexed callback table. Supporting header changes clarified HSD_GObj::obj_kind as unsigned, introduced HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE as 0xFF, gave func_80390A70 a typed prototype, and changed struct r13_EntityPointers from filler bytes to an HSD_GObj* x0[8] array.
what_changed: Deleted the 109-line assembly implementation of gobjobject from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjobject.s.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjobject.c with C implementations for func_80390A3C, func_80390A70, func_80390ADC, and func_80390B0C.
Changed obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjobject.c.o instead of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjobject.s.o.
Changed HSD_GObj::obj_kind from s8 to u8, matching comparisons and stores against 0xFF in the original assembly.
Updated func_80390A70 prototype in include/functions.h from an untyped declaration to void func_80390A70(struct _HSD_GObj*, u8, void*).
Changed struct r13_EntityPointers::x0_filler[0x20] into HSD_GObj* x0[8], enabling typed indexing into the global entity pointer lists.
smart_moves: Represented the 0xFF sentinel as HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE instead of leaving magic constants in object attach/detach logic.
Typed obj_kind as u8, which matches the assembly's lbz/stb behavior and allows HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE to compare cleanly as 0xFF.
Recovered the r13_EntityPointers leading region as an array of eight HSD_GObj* entries, matching the assembly's index-by-arg1 times four access pattern.
Modeled func_80390B0C's destructor behavior as lbl_804D7810[gobj->obj_kind](gobj->hsd_obj), preserving the indirect callback-table call from assembly.
Kept func_80390A70's assert at line 42 with the recovered condition gobj->obj_kind == HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE, matching the string evidence in the removed assembly.
decomp_lessons: When assembly indexes a small-data global by an 8-bit argument shifted left by two, check whether an apparent filler region is actually a pointer array; here x0_filler[0x20] became HSD_GObj* x0[8].
Byte fields loaded with lbz and compared to 0xFF should often be unsigned u8 rather than s8, especially when 0xFF is a valid sentinel enum-like value.
Replace repeated sentinel constants with named defines once a field's lifecycle is understood; HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE documents the empty object state used by attach, detach, and destroy routines.
Assert string literals in deleted assembly can directly identify source conditions and line numbers; the removed data strings supported assert_line(42, gobj->obj_kind == HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE).
After matching a unit, update the build list from asm object to src C object in obj_files.mk as part of the decomp handoff.
naming_conventions: Used the HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE macro name for the 0xFF no-object sentinel.
Used HSD_GObj* for game object pointers and named traversal variable cur in the lookup routine.
Retained unresolved global label names lbl_804D782C and lbl_804D7810 because the PR evidence did not rename them.
The recovered r13_EntityPointers array was named x0, preserving offset-oriented naming while improving the type from raw filler.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the func_80390A3C loop from a linked-list traversal using cur->next at offset 0x08 and classifier comparison at offset 0x00 into a for loop over lbl_804D782C->x0[arg1].
Matched object attach by asserting obj_kind is HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE, then storing the kind byte and object pointer into HSD_GObj fields.
Matched object detach by checking obj_kind != HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE, resetting obj_kind to 0xFF, returning the old hsd_obj pointer, and clearing hsd_obj to NULL.
Matched object destruction by loading a function pointer from lbl_804D7810 indexed by obj_kind and calling it with hsd_obj before clearing the object's kind and pointer.
Used typed prototypes and struct fields to eliminate assembly while preserving small-data global access patterns.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for renames of func_80390A3C, func_80390A70, func_80390ADC, and func_80390B0C to semantic HSD_GObj object API names.
Search later PRs for semantic names replacing lbl_804D782C and lbl_804D7810.
Check whether HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE becomes part of an enum or broader object-kind naming scheme in later baselib work.
Look for additional struct r13_EntityPointers refinements beyond x0[8], x20_fighterGObj, and x24_itemGObj.
